Title: createWishlist — Happy Path
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated (ORG_USER_EMAIL from .env). Creates a wishlist with unique name then deletes it in the final step (inline cleanup).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=setup_me] errors[] empty
[DATA label=setup_me] data.me.id is non-null
[ERRORS label=create_list] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_list] data.createWishlist.id is non-empty GUID
[DATA label=create_list] data.createWishlist.name = AGENT-TEST-WL-001
[COUNT label=create_list] data.createWishlist.itemsCount = 0
[ERRORS label=cleanup_remove] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=create_list] re-query wishlist(listId: {{LIST_ID}}) before cleanup to verify persistence (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; createWishlist returns null id; name mismatch; itemsCount != 0 for empty list
Cleanup: none — cleanup mutation inline at end of Steps

Title: createWishlist — With Description
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Note: sharingKey is platform-generated (not user-settable) and requires scope=AnyoneAnonymous — this case focuses on description only.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=create_list] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_list] data.createWishlist.id is non-empty GUID
[DATA label=create_list] data.createWishlist.name = AGENT-TEST-WL-002
[DATA label=create_list] data.createWishlist.description = Test wishlist with description
[ERRORS label=cleanup_remove] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=create_list] query wishlist(listId) { description } → matches (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; description missing or truncated; id null

Title: changeWishlist — Rename List
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER creates a list then renames it via changeWishlist.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=rename] errors[] empty
[DATA label=rename] data.changeWishlist.name = AGENT-TEST-WL-003-RENAMED
[ERRORS label=verify] errors[] empty
[DATA label=verify] data.wishlist.name = AGENT-TEST-WL-003-RENAMED
[ERRORS label=cleanup_remove] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=verify] confirms rename persisted across mutation boundary
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; name not updated; changeWishlist returns old name; verify query shows stale data

Title: cloneWishlist — Verify Items Copied
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures real product GUID, creates source wishlist with 1 item, then clones it. KNOWN BUG (verified 2026-03-30): cloneWishlist injects phantom product 5a78d778 into every clone, itemsCount=2 instead of 1. This test asserts source preservation + source product in clone; logs clone itemsCount for bug tracking.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_item] data.addWishlistItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=clone] errors[] empty
[DATA label=clone] data.cloneWishlist.id is non-empty GUID
[DATA label=clone] data.cloneWishlist.name = AGENT-TEST-WL-004-CLONE
[COUNT label=clone] data.cloneWishlist.itemsCount >= 1
[ERRORS label=verify_src] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=verify_src] data.wishlist.itemsCount = 1
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] data.cloneWishlist.itemsCount: expected 1, KNOWN BUG yields 2 (phantom product 5a78d778 'MAMMOET SHOT GLASS BOSTON SHOT 2.5CL BOX'). Review evidence JSON.
[ROUNDTRIP label=verify_src] source list unchanged after clone
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; source list destroyed by clone; clone has 0 items; source product missing from clone

Title: removeWishlist — Verify Deleted
Preconditions: Runner-native. Creates wishlist, removes it, verifies subsequent query returns null.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=remove_list] errors[] empty
[DATA label=remove_list] data.removeWishlist = true
[DATA label=verify_gone] data.wishlist is null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] removeWishlist returns Boolean (no sub-selection allowed — {id} on Boolean would be DV-010)
[ROUNDTRIP label=verify_gone] list no longer queryable post-remove
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on remove; removeWishlist returns false; list still queryable after delete

Title: removeWishlist — Non-Existent ID Returns False Or Error
Preconditions: Runner-native. Attempts to remove a zero-GUID wishlist. Server should return structured result (false or error — either is acceptable), no 500 or crash.
Steps:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP remove_bogus]
  mutation { removeWishlist(command: { listId: "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000" }) }
[GQL-EXEC remove_bogus]
Assertions: [DATA label=remove_bogus] data is non-null or errors[] reports not-found
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Either errors[] contains descriptive not-found message OR data.removeWishlist = false (graceful not-found). No HTTP 500 or stack traces in response.
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; unhandled exception; stack trace leaks in errors[].message; server crash

Title: wishlist Query — Get by listId
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures real product GUID, creates wishlist + adds 1 item, then queries back to verify all fields resolve.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=query_list] errors[] empty
[DATA label=query_list] data.wishlist.id = {{LIST_ID}}
[DATA label=query_list] data.wishlist.name = AGENT-TEST-WL-007
[COUNT label=query_list] data.wishlist.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=query_list] data.wishlist.items.0.productId is non-null
[COUNT label=query_list] data.wishlist.items.0.quantity = 2
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=query_list] re-execute same query → identical response
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; wishlist null; itemsCount mismatch; quantity wrong

Title: wishlists Query — List with Pagination
Preconditions: Runner-native. Queries user's wishlists with first=5 pagination. Asserts structural invariants — totalCount may be 0 if none exist (acceptable).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=list_wl] errors[] empty
[DATA label=list_wl] data.wishlists is non-null
[COUNT label=list_wl] data.wishlists.totalCount >= 0
[DATA label=list_wl] data.wishlists.items is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=list_wl] query is deterministic — re-execution returns same totalCount (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; totalCount negative or missing; items array null

Title: sharedWishlist Query — Public Access via Platform-Generated Key
Preconditions: Not runner-automatable — requires navigating storefront UI ({{FRONT_URL}}/account/lists) to obtain platform-generated <SHARING_KEY> UUID. sharingKey is NOT user-settable via API (custom strings silently ignored — only scope=AnyoneAnonymous triggers platform generation). Kept as Manual until runner gains UI-step primitive.
Steps: Manual-only. See legacy suite 050h for storefront steps: (1) create wishlist with scope=AnyoneAnonymous, (2) navigate to /account/lists, (3) extract sharing UUID from shared-list card URL, (4) sharedWishlist(sharingKey: <UUID>) query — public (no auth needed).
Assertions: [MANUAL] All assertions verified by tester in GraphiQL after extracting sharing key from storefront UI
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Manual case only
Failure_Signals: sharedWishlist returns null for platform key; scope not Organization; auth required on shared query
Cleanup: Manual — executed via storefront UI + GraphiQL

Title: addWishlistItem — Single Item
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures a real product GUID from products query (B2B virtual catalog fc596540...), creates wishlist, adds one item, verifies itemsCount increments and product matches.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=setup_prod] errors[] empty
[DATA label=setup_prod] data.products.items.0.id is non-null
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_item] data.addWishlistItem.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=add_item] data.addWishlistItem.items.0.productId = {{PRODUCT_ID}}
[COUNT label=add_item] data.addWishlistItem.items.0.quantity = 1
[DATA label=add_item] data.addWishlistItem.items.0.id is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=add_item] query wishlist(listId) → items[0] matches addWishlistItem response
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount not incremented; items[] empty; productId or quantity mismatch

Title: addWishlistItems — Multiple Items in One Call
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures 2 real product GUIDs from products query, bulk-adds both.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_items] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_items] data.addWishlistItems.itemsCount = 2
[COUNT label=add_items] data.addWishlistItems.items.length = 2
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] items[].productId includes both seeds (PROD_HEADPHONES + PROD_LAPTOP) — verify evidence JSON
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount != 2; partial insert; duplicate insertions

Title: updateWishListItems — Change Item Quantity
Preconditions: Runner-native. NOTE: mutation name is updateWishListItems (capital L — schema drift trap). Captures real product GUID, changes qty 1 → 5.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=update_qty] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=update_qty] data.updateWishListItems.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=update_qty] data.updateWishListItems.items.0.id = {{LINE_ITEM_ID}}
[COUNT label=update_qty] data.updateWishListItems.items.0.quantity = 5
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=update_qty] wishlist(listId) → items[0].quantity = 5 (persistence check)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; quantity still 1; updateWishlistItems (lowercase l) treated as unknown field (DV-009)

Title: removeWishlistItem — Single Item
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures real product GUID, creates list, adds 1 item, removes it by lineItemId, verifies itemsCount drops to 0.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=remove_item] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=remove_item] data.removeWishlistItem.itemsCount = 0
[COUNT label=remove_item] data.removeWishlistItem.items.length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=remove_item] list still exists (wishlist query returns it) but is empty
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount not decremented; removed item still in items[]; wishlist itself deleted (null) instead of emptied

Title: removeWishlistItems — Bulk Remove
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures 2 real product GUIDs, bulk-adds them, then removes both via removeWishlistItems.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=bulk_remove] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=bulk_remove] data.removeWishlistItems.itemsCount = 0
[COUNT label=bulk_remove] data.removeWishlistItems.items.length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=bulk_remove] wishlist(listId) confirms empty
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; partial removal; itemsCount = 1; list null post-remove

Title: moveWishlistItem — Between Two Lists
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures real product GUID, creates 2 wishlists, adds 1 item to source, moves it to dest, verifies source empty and dest populated.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=move] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=move] data.moveWishlistItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=verify_dest] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=verify_dest] data.wishlist.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=verify_dest] data.wishlist.items.0.productId is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=move] moveWishlistItem returns the DESTINATION wishlist state (itemsCount=1 after move). verify_dest also confirms destination=1.
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; item remains in source; missing from destination; duplicated in both

Title: moveWishlistItem — Non-Existent Destination
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures real product GUID, attempts to move to zero-GUID destination. Expects structured error or graceful failure — no data loss on source.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=verify_src] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=verify_src] data.wishlist.itemsCount = 1
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] move_bad response: either errors[] non-empty with descriptive message OR data returned — but source MUST still have the item (no data loss)
[ROUNDTRIP label=verify_src] source item preserved
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; source item deleted; stack trace in errors[].message; silent success on invalid move

Title: createCartFromWishlist — Cart Populated
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures real product GUID, creates wishlist with 1 item, calls createCartFromWishlist, verifies cart is populated with the same items.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=cart_from_wl] errors[] empty
[DATA label=cart_from_wl] data.createCartFromWishlist.id is non-null
[COUNT label=cart_from_wl] data.createCartFromWishlist.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=cart_from_wl] data.createCartFromWishlist.items.0.productId is non-null
[COUNT label=cart_from_wl] data.createCartFromWishlist.items.0.quantity = 1
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=cart_from_wl] cart(storeId, currencyCode) returns same items (runner-manual)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; cart.itemsCount = 0; items[] empty; cart.id null; productId mismatch
Cleanup: none — wishlist removed inline; cart is user-owned default cart (no API cart-delete needed)

Title: createCartFromWishlist — Empty Wishlist Behavior
Preconditions: Runner-native. Calls createCartFromWishlist on an empty wishlist. Server may return (a) errors[] with descriptive message or (b) an empty cart — either is acceptable.
Steps:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP setup_me]
  query { me { id } }
[GQL-EXEC setup_me]
[GQL-CAPTURE setup_me.data.me.id → USER_ID]
[GQL-OP create_list]
  mutation { createWishlist(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" listName: "AGENT-TEST-WL-018-EMPTY" }) { id } }
[GQL-EXEC create_list]
[GQL-CAPTURE create_list.data.createWishlist.id → LIST_ID]
[GQL-OP cart_empty]
  mutation { createCartFromWishlist(command: { listId: "{{LIST_ID}}" }) { id itemsCount } }
[GQL-EXEC cart_empty]
[GQL-OP cleanup_remove]
  mutation { removeWishlist(command: { listId: "{{LIST_ID}}" }) }
[GQL-EXEC cleanup_remove]
Assertions: [ERRORS label=cleanup_remove] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Either: errors[] non-empty with descriptive message on empty input OR data.createCartFromWishlist returns a cart with itemsCount=0. Document which branch observed.
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; stack trace in errors[]; server crash; silent null cart creation

Title: addWishlistItem — Without Authentication
Preconditions: Runner-native. No [AUTH] block — attempts addWishlistItem anonymously. Expects authentication error or silent null.
Assertions: [DATA label=add_noauth] data.addWishlistItem is null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Either errors[] non-empty with auth/authorization message OR data.addWishlistItem null. No internal IDs, stack traces, or SQL hints in errors[].
Failure_Signals: Mutation succeeds without auth (data returned); HTTP 500; unhandled exception; stack trace leaks in errors[]

Title: wishlist Query — Cross-User Access Denial
Preconditions: Two-user scenario — requires a second role alias (USER2 — personal account in aliases.json). ORG_USER creates the list then USER2 attempts to read it.
Steps:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP setup_me]
  query { me { id } }
[GQL-EXEC setup_me]
[GQL-CAPTURE setup_me.data.me.id → USER_ID]
[GQL-OP create_list]
  mutation { createWishlist(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" listName: "AGENT-TEST-WL-020" }) { id } }
[GQL-EXEC create_list]
[GQL-CAPTURE create_list.data.createWishlist.id → LIST_ID]
[AUTH role=USER2]
[GQL-OP cross_read]
  query { wishlist(listId: "{{LIST_ID}}") { id name } }
[GQL-EXEC cross_read]
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP cleanup_remove]
  mutation { removeWishlist(command: { listId: "{{LIST_ID}}" }) }
[GQL-EXEC cleanup_remove]
Assertions:
[DATA label=cross_read] data.wishlist is null — private list not exposed to other users
[ERRORS label=cleanup_remove] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Either: errors[] non-empty with access-denied message OR data.wishlist is null. Both acceptable (hard-gate or silent-null). No private list data leaked.
Failure_Signals: USER2 receives private list data (privacy violation); HTTP 500; stack trace

Title: Personal Account — Private Wishlist OK But Cannot See Org Lists
Preconditions: Runner-native. USER2 is a personal account (no org membership). Creates a private wishlist, then attempts to read an ORG_USER's org-scope list — should be denied.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=personal_me] errors[] empty
[DATA label=personal_me] data.me.contact.organizationId is null
[ERRORS label=create_personal_list] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_personal_list] data.createWishlist.id is non-empty GUID
[DATA label=personal_reads_org] data.wishlist is null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] personal_reads_org: either errors[] with access-denied OR data.wishlist null. Both acceptable.
[EVIDENCE] personal_me.contact.organizationId confirmed null — USER2 is genuinely unaffiliated
Failure_Signals: USER2 has org_id set (not a personal account); personal user reads org-scope list successfully (isolation failure)
